title: trymalium myrtillus subsp. myrtillus
intro: Trymalium myrtillus subsp. myrtillus, also known as bilberry, is a subspecies of the trymalium myrtillus plant, a type of shrub that produces small, dark blue or purple berries. The medicinal properties of bilberry include its high content of anthocyanins, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of bilberry consumption include improved cardiovascular health, enhanced vision, and protection against age-related diseases such as Alzheimer's and Parkinson's. Bilberry can be used in various herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, capsules, and jams, to support overall health and well-being.
